evals.name: audit-security-and-conflicts
evals.prompt: I've collected a few skills in /sessions/serene-dreamy-ritchie/skill-evals/fixtures/installed-skills and I'm a little nervous about them. Can you check whether any are a security risk and whether any of them overlap or step on each other?
evals.expected_output: Runs skill_doctor.py audit on the fixture dir. Flags quick-deploy as high-risk (curl-pipe-bash, hard-coded ghp_ token, env-var exfiltration), and identifies the meeting-notes-pro vs meeting-recap trigger conflict. Leads with the security risk, shows file/line evidence, and frames flags as 'worth a look' rather than proof of malice.

assertions.text: Names quick-deploy as the security-risk skill
Identifies at least one concrete red flag (curl|bash, hard-coded token, or env-var exfiltration)
Identifies the meeting-notes-pro vs meeting-recap trigger overlap
Runs the bundled skill_doctor.py tool rather than hand-rolling the analysis

evals.name: which-fires-debug-triggering
evals.prompt: When I ask my agent to 'summarize this meeting' it sometimes gives me the wrong format — I think two of my skills are fighting over it. The skills are in /sessions/serene-dreamy-ritchie/skill-evals/fixtures/installed-skills. Which one actually fires for that, and what should I do about it?
evals.expected_output: Runs skill_doctor.py which "summarize this meeting". Reports that meeting-notes-pro and meeting-recap both match and the choice is ambiguous, and recommends tightening one skill's description so they stop competing.

assertions.text: Identifies both meeting-notes-pro and meeting-recap as competing for the prompt
Flags the match as ambiguous / a near-tie
Recommends disambiguating or tightening one skill's description
